title: Biolistic Transformation of Pseudo-nitzschia multistriata and Pseudo-nitzschia arenysensis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"99ndv","text":"This protocol allows transformation of two species of Pseudo-nitzschia through biolistic method.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":54,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f1ecb","text":"With this method it is possible to insert one (transformation) or two (co-transformation) plasmids in the cells. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare plasmid with your gene of interest at the concentration of 1 µg ml -1 .
Grow wild-type cells in f/2 medium up to mid log-phase.
Prepare f/2 plates (0.4% agarose in f/2 medium) and leave to dry under hood.
Count the cells. For each plate between 3-5x10 7 cells are needed.
Centrifuge the cells at 2465 g and resuspend the pellet in a very small volume of f/2 medium.
Spread the cells onto agarose plates. Leave to dry under hood.
DNA precipitation is carried out using Tungsten M-10 microcarrier 0.7 µm particles (BioRad).
Dilute the tungsten particles in a final concentration of 3 mg in 50 µl of water for plate.

Add to particles 5 µl of plasmid (1 µg µl -1 ) (for the transformation) or 3 µl of each plasmid (1 µg µl -1 ) (for the co-transformation), 50 µl 2.5 M CaCl 2 , and 20 µl of 0.1 M spermidine. Continue vortexing for 3 min, then microfuge for 10 sec and remove supernatant. Wash with 250 µl 100% ethanol, microfuge again, then resuspend in 60 µl ethanol. Keep tin ice.
Wash rupture disks and macrocarriers in ethanol and allow to dry.

Bombardment is carried out in a Biorad PDS-1000/He microprojectile accelerator. Shooting is carried out using 1,550 PSI rupture disks. Plates for transformation are placed in the middle slot immediately under the macrocarrier.
10µl aliquot of the prepared particles were transferred to the centre of each macrocarrier. Allow to dry.
Keep the plates in incubator overnight.
Resuspend the cells (each plate) in 200 ml of f/2 medium with antibiotic. Pseudo-nitzschia cells grow better in liquid medium.
Usually untransformed Pseudo-nitzschia cells are dead at 10-12 days post-application of antibiotic.
creator: Valeria Sabatino
